opp.name: Metso ANA May'26
opp.next_step: Marc has setup a follow up call with Julio in the last week of May, he said he will get some one from his Procurement COE team
opp.ais_why:
* Metso is at an early, exploratory stage: the next step is a follow-up where the prospect "will get someone from his Procurement COE team" - the evaluation has not been scoped yet.
* The deal is an ANA/analytics-led opportunity with Merlin and Agentic AI in scope, so AI is within the intended footprint.
* AI/analytics modules are in scope (Merlin, Agentic AI, ANA) but no customer-side AI interest is evidenced for this opportunity; Qualified at 10%.

sources: SF Q2/Q3 synthesis read: Customer_Business_Problem__c, Business_Objectives__c, Value_to_Customer__c, Compelling_Event__c all null
Avoma: zero calls discovered (opp+account+attendee union)
Products field: Merlin;Agentic AI;ANA (product scope only, no validated business case)
metrics.narrative: No quantified business problem, cost impact, or ROI case has been captured. Salesforce fields Customer_Business_Problem__c, Business_Objectives__c, Value_to_Customer__c, and Compelling_Event__c are unpopulated (read via Q2/Q3 synthesis queries). No Avoma calls exist to surface buyer-stated pain or metrics. The only scoping signal is the Products field (Merlin, Agentic AI, ANA), which implies procurement/sourcing automation and agentic AI interest, but this is a product list, not a validated business case. Without a discovery call or documented pain, we have no evidence of what operational or financial problem Metso is solving, no baseline metrics (current cost, cycle time, error rate, headcount), and no quantified outcome the buyer expects. The next move is to conduct discovery with Julio and the Procurement COE colleague he promised to bring, and extract at minimum: current procurement tool stack, pain points (manual work, supplier risk, spend visibility, contract compliance), and a quantified target (% cost savings, days saved per cycle, FTE reduction).

sources: SF Q3: X10b_Champion_Business_Buyer__c, Decision_Maker_Name_Title__c null
Contact Roles: Julio Gastiaburu (Sourcing Manager), no Champion role, last activity 2026-05-27 (39 days ago)
NextStep (overdue): Julio to reschedule late-May call, no follow-up logged
Avoma: zero calls to assess advocacy or engagement
champion.narrative: No champion has been identified or developed. Salesforce fields X10b_Champion_Business_Buyer__c, Decision_Maker_Name_Title__c are unpopulated (Q3 read), and no Contact Role of Champion exists. Julio Gastiaburu (Sourcing Manager, the only contact) has shown no evidence of advocacy: he agreed to a follow-up call in late May and promised to bring a Procurement COE colleague, but the call never occurred, he has not re-engaged in 39 days, and there is no record of him pushing Zycus internally, sharing competitive intel, or making introductions upward. No Avoma calls exist to assess his enthusiasm, access to the Economic Buyer/Decision Maker, or willingness to coach us. His engagement is passive at best. The next move is to re-engage Julio, assess his interest and influence ('Are you driving this evaluation internally? Who else should I be talking to?'), and if he is not a champion, ask him to introduce us to the person who is (likely his manager or the Procurement COE lead). If Julio is unwilling or unable to facilitate upward access, escalate to the deal owner's manager (Alexa Bradley) to activate a peer relationship at VP level.

competition.narrative: No competitive intelligence has been captured. Salesforce fields Competitors__c, Others_Competitors_Please_specify__c, How_are_you_addressing_your_problem_toda__c, Existing_vendor__c, Replacing_What__c are unpopulated (Q3 read). No Avoma calls, Next_Step__c/Next_Step_History__c mentions, or completed Task subjects reference a competitor (Coupa, SAP Ariba, Jaggaer, GEP, Ivalua, or others). We do not know if Metso is benchmarking multiple vendors, sole-sourcing Zycus, locked into an existing platform, or addressing procurement pain manually/with internal tools. The Products field (Merlin, Agentic AI, ANA) suggests AI differentiation may be the wedge, but without knowing the competitive set or Metso's current stack, we cannot position effectively. The next move is discovery with Julio: 'How are you solving this today? Are you evaluating other vendors, and if so, who? What would success look like in comparison?' Document the answers, identify the wedge (likely Merlin agentic negotiation if Metso is benchmarking traditional procurement suites), and prepare a competitive review.

Avoma: zero calls

identify_pain.narrative: No specific operational or financial pain has been articulated by the buyer. Salesforce fields Customer_Business_Problem__c, Pain_points_in_the_current_solution__c, What_if_this_is_not_done__c, Compelling_Event__c are unpopulated (Q2/Q3 read). No Avoma calls exist to capture buyer-stated pain. The only pain inference comes from the Products field (Merlin, Agentic AI, ANA), which implies Metso may be seeking to automate procurement workflows, improve sourcing analytics, or deploy AI-driven negotiation, but this is speculative. We do not know if the pain is manual sourcing processes, poor spend visibility, supplier risk, slow contract negotiation, compliance gaps, or something else, and we do not know who owns the pain (Procurement COE, Finance, Business Unit leaders). Without a pain owner and a quantified pain (% of time wasted, $ of leakage, compliance incidents per quarter), we cannot build a compelling business case or urgency. The next move is discovery with Julio: 'What problem are you trying to solve, and what is it costing you today (time, money, risk)? What happens if you do not solve it this year?' Capture the pain and the pain owner.

sources: SF Q2: Current_Contract_Expiration__c null
SF Q3: no paper-process fields populated
Avoma: zero calls
paper_process.narrative: No contracting or implementation mechanics have been captured. Salesforce field Current_Contract_Expiration__c is null (Q2 read), and no Avoma calls exist to discuss MSA/SOW requirements, legal review, procurement channel (direct vs via SI/partner), InfoSec requirements, or ERP/system integration scope. We do not know if Metso has an existing procurement platform under contract (and whether this is a replacement, an add-on, or a new category), whether an SI (Accenture, Deloitte, etc.) is involved, or whether Metso's legal/InfoSec teams impose specific SLA, data residency, or AI transparency requirements common in industrial Fortune 500s. The next move is to ask Julio in discovery: 'What is your current procurement tool stack, and are you under contract with any vendor? Do you work with an SI or implementation partner? What does your legal/InfoSec approval process look like for a SaaS AI tool?' Document the answers to anticipate gating and timeline impact.

sources: SF Q3 synthesis: X10a_Sponsor__c, Who_will_approve_budget__c, Purchase_approvals_Required_from__c, Executive_Sponsor_Identified__c all null
Contact Roles: 1 contact, Julio Gastiaburu (Sourcing Manager), no EB/Exec Sponsor role
Avoma: zero calls
NextStep (last week of May, overdue): Julio to bring Procurement COE colleague (identity unknown, call did not occur)
economic_buyer.narrative: No Economic Buyer identified. Salesforce fields X10a_Sponsor__c, Who_will_approve_budget__c, Purchase_approvals_Required_from__c, and Executive_Sponsor_Identified__c are all unpopulated (Q3 synthesis read). No Contact Role of Economic Buyer or Executive Sponsor exists. The single known stakeholder is Julio Gastiaburu, a Sourcing Manager (mid-level), who has no budget authority for a $110k AI/Merlin deal. No Avoma calls exist to reveal who controls commercial or pricing conversations. The NextStep field indicates Julio will bring 'someone from his Procurement COE team' to the late-May call, but that colleague was never named, the call never occurred, and there is no evidence this person is an EB. For a Merlin/ANA deal of this size at Metso (a global industrial conglomerate), the EB is likely a VP or Head of Procurement, possibly CFO involvement given AI investment scrutiny. The next move is to ask Julio directly in the rescheduled call: 'Who will approve the budget and contract for this?' and 'Who do you report to, and should they be part of this conversation?' Then secure an introduction upward.

decision_process.narrative: No decision process has been mapped. Salesforce fields What_is_the_decision_process__c, Mandate__c, Does_the_Buyer_need_approval__c, Multiple_approvals__c are unpopulated (Q3 read). No Avoma calls exist to reveal whether Metso is running an RFP, a structured vendor shortlist, or an informal evaluation, and no evidence of the approval chain (Procurement COE, CFO, IT, Legal, InfoSec). The only process clue is the NextStep field (Julio to bring a Procurement COE colleague to a late-May call), which implies a COE exists and may gate vendor selection, but the call never happened and the colleague's role is unknown. We do not know if this is a centralized procurement decision, a business-unit pilot, or a global rollout. The next move is to ask Julio in the rescheduled call: 'Walk me through your evaluation and approval process. Who else is involved, and what are the key gates (RFP response, demo, shortlist, commercial proposal, legal/InfoSec review, final sign-off)? What is the timeline for each?' Map the process and identify where Zycus needs to influence.

The Products field (Merlin, Agentic AI, ANA) suggests interest in procurement automation and agentic negotiation/analytics, but we have no evidence of whether this is an RFP-driven evaluation, a sole-source exploration, or a benchmarking exercise, and no evidence of what criteria matter most (AI capability vs cost vs speed to value vs existing-vendor lock-in). The next move is discovery: ask Julio 'What are the top 3-5 capabilities you are evaluating across vendors?' and 'Is this an RFP process, and if so, can you share the evaluation scorecard or key decision factors?' Document the answers and map them to Zycus strengths.

cro_panel.intro: One read per score, in language a CRO/CO can act on. We're behind on this one — the reads below say why and what would change it.

blocks.read: We're well behind — this one is cold.
blocks.title: Zycus win position

bullets.full: Losing on capability fit — No AIS_Score__c, AIS_Status__c, or AIS_Why__c populated (Q2 read). Products field lists Merlin, Agentic AI, and ANA, which implies AI interest and agentic-negotiation scope, but this is a product list, not a validated AI appetite signal. No Avoma calls exist to assess buyer AI maturity, risk tolerance, or agentic-automation receptivity. Tier assignment: AI Curious (inferred from Products scope, but unconfirmed). Next move: discovery call to validate AI appetite and position Merlin agentic negotiation as the wedge.
bullets.text: Losing on capability fit — No AIS_Score__c, AIS_Status__c, or AIS_Why__c populated (Q2 read).

bullets.full: Buyer is leaning elsewhere — Products field lists Merlin, Agentic AI, and ANA, signaling strong AI/Merlin scope and potential AI appetite, but this is unvalidated (no AIS_Score__c, AIS_Status__c, or AIS_Why__c populated; no Avoma calls to confirm buyer AI maturity, use-case alignment, or agentic-negotiation interest). Without discovery, we do not know if Metso's AI appetite is genuine (seeking cutting-edge agentic procurement), aspirational (exploring AI but risk-averse), or checkbox-driven (RFP includes AI but not a priority). The Products scope suggests AI positioning IS the strategy, but buyer receptivity, competitive AI messaging, and readiness for Merlin deployment are completely unknown. Under-positioned risk: if Metso is AI-curious but Marc has not articulated the Merlin agentic negotiation wedge and I2O intelligence value, we may be losing to a competitor with simpler, safer messaging. Next move: discovery call to assess AI maturity ('What is your experience with AI in procurement? What outcomes do you expect from agentic automation?') and position Merlin's supplier-price-history negotiation agent as the Phase 1 proof point.

blocks.title: What moves it forward

commentary.deal_risk: Risk 14 is driven by Verdict 'At Risk' — weak forward intent. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.win_position: Win 0 reflects a weak position: momentum 41 vs stage-expected 50. Offsetting it: momentum 41 vs stage-expected 50.
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 41 (slipping backward): Engagement cadence dropped (pulse cooling). Eased toward flat — 39d quiet (>30d window); momentum down 6pt (stalling).
commentary.evidence_coverage: Early Read: 3 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (engagement, stage integrity, commercial). Reduced because the most recent contact is stale (recency ×0.41).
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment 44 reflects real customer investment: Security/procurement review underway; Customer owns 1 open commitment(s).
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 0 is anchored on the stage win (0), nudged by commitment 44 and momentum 41, and dragged by risk 14. Read is Early Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.

items.sentiment: Neutral (passive engagement; agreed to follow-up call in late May but has not re-engaged in 39 days; no evidence of advocacy or internal push)

items.detail: Promised late-May follow-up call is 30+ days overdue; buyer has not re-engaged and Marc has logged no follow-up attempt. Deal is cooling after a single interaction.

Single-threaded to a mid-level Sourcing Manager (Julio) with no visibility into Economic Buyer, Decision Maker, or senior procurement/finance sponsorship. No multi-threading despite 57 days in Qualified.

Zero discovery: no calls held, no MEDDPICC captured, no competitive intelligence, no pain/metrics quantified, no decision process mapped. Entire deal hypothesis (AI/Merlin fit, buyer urgency, competitive positioning) is unvalidated.

No Economic Buyer or Decision Maker identified; single-threaded to mid-level Sourcing Manager (Julio) with no visibility into senior procurement/finance/CFO sponsorship required for $110k AI/Merlin deal. Risk: deal stalls at Julio's level or is deprioritized without executive advocate.

Cooling engagement: 39 days since last buyer touch; promised late-May follow-up call is 30+ days overdue with no reattempt logged. Risk: deal going dark, buyer has moved on or is prioritizing another vendor.

Zero discovery: no calls held, no MEDDPICC captured, no pain quantified, no competitive intel, no decision process mapped. Entire deal hypothesis (AI/Merlin fit, buyer urgency, competitive positioning) is unvalidated. Risk: deal is speculative, not real demand.

Single point of contact (Julio) has disengaged and provided no multi-threading path despite promising to bring Procurement COE colleague. Risk: no alternate route into the account if Julio remains unresponsive; deal blocked by single-thread failure.

items.trigger: Promised late-May follow-up call with Julio + Procurement COE colleague is 30+ days overdue; 39 days since last buyer touch (2026-05-27); no follow-up attempt logged.
items.expected_effect: Restarts buyer engagement within 72 hours; secures discovery call with Julio and Procurement COE colleague within 2 weeks; prevents deal from going dark. If Julio unresponsive, VP-level escalation opens alternate route into account and validates whether deal is real.

items.action: Conduct full discovery call with Julio and Procurement COE colleague (once rescheduled): extract business problem, quantified pain (current cost/cycle time/risk), decision criteria (top 5 functional/AI requirements), decision process (RFP timeline, approval chain, gates), current stack (existing vendor, contract expiration), competitive set (who else are they evaluating), and Economic Buyer identity (who approves budget). Document in Salesforce MEDDPICC fields and position Merlin agentic negotiation as the Phase 1 wedge.
items.trigger: Zero discovery depth: no Avoma calls, no Customer_Business_Problem__c, Business_Objectives__c, Pain, Metrics, Decision Criteria/Process, or Competitive intel captured. Products scoped (Merlin, ANA, Agentic AI) but buyer fit and urgency unvalidated.
items.expected_effect: Validates deal hypothesis (AI/Merlin fit, buyer urgency, procurement pain). Quantifies business case (ROI input). Maps MEDDPICC coverage (EB, DM, Champion, Criteria, Process, Pain, Competition). Enables defensible forecast and ranked next moves. Derisk speculative-deal vulnerability.

items.expected_effect: Identifies Economic Buyer and secures access to senior procurement/finance decision-maker. Multi-threads deal to reduce single-point-of-failure risk. Enables EB-level value conversation and accelerates decision process. Derisk executive-alignment vulnerability.

items.action: Assess and develop champion: in discovery call, evaluate Julio's interest, internal influence, and access to EB/DM. If passive or low-influence, ask 'Who internally is driving this procurement AI initiative, and can you introduce me?' Identify the real champion (likely Procurement COE lead or VP Procurement) and build advocacy through value alignment (quantified ROI, AI roadmap fit, reference customers). If no champion emerges, escalate to deal owner's manager (Alexa Bradley) for executive sponsor cultivation.

Secures advocate who will push Zycus through procurement evaluation, share competitive intel, and navigate internal politics. Derisk champion-strength and political vulnerabilities. Accelerates deal progression to the relevant stakeholder.

confidence_signals.summary: Deal is cooling and unvalidated. Negative signals: 39 days since last buyer touch, overdue follow-up with no reattempt, single-threaded to disengaged mid-level contact, zero discovery calls, no MEDDPICC coverage, no pain quantified, no competitive intel, Products scoped but buyer fit unconfirmed. Positive signal: 300 days to close provides recovery runway if re-engaged within 2 weeks. Overall: low engagement, high uncertainty, recoverable if immediate action taken.

north_star_verdict.math: Days to close: 300 (close 2027-05-01). Time in Qualified stage: 58 days since creation (2026-05-08). Last buyer activity: 2026-05-27, 39 days ago. No forward slip observed (close date unchanged). Qualified-stage deals of this size typically take 90-120 days to progress to Formal Evaluation; at 58 days in-stage the deal is within normal rhythm IF actively worked, but the 39-day silence and overdue follow-up indicate stall, not natural pacing. Required pace to close by May 2027: discovery and demo by Sept 2026, RFI/RFP by Dec 2026, shortlist by Feb 2027, commercials/validation by Apr 2027. Current trajectory: behind on discovery (should be complete by now), but recoverable if re-engaged within 2 weeks.

north_star_verdict.headline: Cooling deal 39 days since last buyer touch; promised late-May follow-up is 30+ days overdue with no reattempt logged. Single-threaded to mid-level sourcing with zero discovery depth (no calls, no MEDDPICC, no pain quantified, no competitive intel). 300 days to close provides runway, but momentum has stalled and the deal hypothesis (AI/Merlin fit, urgency, buying process) remains completely unvalidated.

flags: Re-engage immediately to restart momentum: the promised late-May follow-up call with Julio and the Procurement COE colleague is 30 days overdue with no logged follow-up from the relevant stakeholder is a brand-new, early-stage deal that will die if the buyer goes silent. Marc should reach out within 48 hours with a value-add nudge (a peer reference, a Merlin use-case one-pager, or a relevant insight on procurement AI trends) to re-establish the conversation and reschedule the discovery call. If Julio does not reply in 5 business days, escalate to a senior Metso procurement contact (Rami Salminen, Elisa Yates) with a brief, executive-level intro to the Zycus AI/negotiation value prop and ask for a 20-minute exploration call.

Multi-thread beyond Julio immediately to de-risk single-point-of-contact exposure. Julio is a category-level sourcing manager and unlikely to control a $110k budget or drive an enterprise AI/procurement platform evaluation alone. The discovery call must map the buying committee (who else is involved, who controls budget, who approves technology purchases) and secure introductions. Account-level contacts include several senior procurement leaders (Rami Salminen, GM Global Procurement; Elisa Yates, Senior Procurement Manager; Jim Bathie, Director Procurement) and finance executives (Bob Wissing, VP Finance; Kalle Sipila, SVP Finance) who are logical EB/sponsor candidates. Ask Julio to introduce Marc to the Procurement COE lead and the budget owner in the first 14 days, and add those contacts to the opportunity contact roles once confirmed involved.

Validate the AI/Merlin fit hypothesis and position the agentic negotiation story as the wedge if they prove AI-hungry. Products__c (Merlin;Agentic AI;ANA) reflects our AI-forward positioning, but we have not confirmed Metso values negotiation automation, has a negotiation pain Merlin solves, or sees AI as a differentiator vs a nice-to-have. Use the discovery call to probe: are you exploring AI for procurement, what negotiation challenges do you face (cycle time, win-rate, supplier push-back, cost leakage), and how do you currently handle complex multi-round negotiations. If they express AI appetite, lead with the Merlin agentic negotiation narrative (autonomous supplier price analysis, real-time counteroffer generation, learning from historical deals) and tie it to a quantified ROI (e.g. 15–20% faster negotiation cycles, 3–5% better outcomes). If AI is not a priority, reframe ANA as a best-practice sourcing analytics and category management platform and position Merlin as a future-proof add-on.

Cooling engagement: 39 days since last buyer touch (2026-05-27); promised late-May follow-up call with Julio + Procurement COE colleague is 30+ days overdue with no reattempt logged. Risk: deal going dark.

Single-threaded: only 1 contact role (Julio Gastiaburu, Sourcing Manager, mid-level). No Economic Buyer, Decision Maker, Executive Sponsor, or Procurement COE colleague identified. No multi-threading despite $110k Merlin/AI scope requiring senior procurement/finance/IT sponsorship.

Zero discovery depth: no Avoma calls discovered (opp+account+attendee union). No Customer_Business_Problem__c, Pain, Metrics, Compelling_Event, Business_Requirements__c, or Decision Criteria captured. Entire deal hypothesis (AI/Merlin fit, buyer urgency, procurement pain, competitive positioning) is unvalidated.

No MEDDPICC coverage: Economic Buyer unknown, Decision Maker unknown, Champion undeveloped, no quantified Metrics, Decision Criteria/Process/Paper Process unmapped, Pain not articulated, Competition unknown. All synthesis-map fields null.

Products scoped but unvalidated: Merlin, Agentic AI, ANA listed in Products field, but no evidence of buyer use-case alignment, AI maturity expectations, integration requirements, or functional priorities. Scope may be aspirational, not validated demand.
best_practice_check.summary: Deal is cooling and validation-starved. Critical gaps: (1) 39 days since last buyer touch, overdue follow-up with no reattempt; (2) single-threaded to mid-level sourcing, no EB/DM/Champion; (3) zero discovery calls, no MEDDPICC, no pain quantified, no competitive intel; (4) Products scoped (Merlin, ANA, Agentic AI) but buyer fit and urgency completely unvalidated. Immediate action required to re-engage and validate the deal hypothesis before it goes dark.

competitive_position.summary: No competitive intelligence captured. Competitors__c and all related fields (Q3 synthesis map) are null. No Avoma calls, NextStep mentions, or Task descriptions reference a competitor. Current stack (How_are_you_addressing_your_problem_toda__c, Existing_vendor__c) is unknown. Without discovery, we do not know if Metso is sole-sourcing Zycus, benchmarking traditional procurement suites (Coupa, Ariba, Jaggaer, GEP), locked into an incumbent, or solving procurement pain manually. The Products scope (Merlin, Agentic AI, ANA) suggests AI differentiation is the likely wedge if Metso is evaluating legacy platforms, but this is unvalidated. Next move: ask Julio in discovery 'How are you solving this today, and who else are you looking at?' and position Merlin's agentic negotiation and I2O intelligence as the Phase 1 differentiator.

ai_positioning_strength.score: Unknown (Products field shows Merlin+Agentic AI+ANA scope; no AIS fields populated; no calls to validate buyer AI appetite or positioning effectiveness)
ai_positioning_strength.summary: Products field lists Merlin, Agentic AI, and ANA, signaling strong AI/Merlin scope and potential AI appetite, but this is unvalidated (no AIS_Score__c, AIS_Status__c, or AIS_Why__c populated; no Avoma calls to confirm buyer AI maturity, use-case alignment, or agentic-negotiation interest). Without discovery, we do not know if Metso's AI appetite is genuine (seeking cutting-edge agentic procurement), aspirational (exploring AI but risk-averse), or checkbox-driven (RFP includes AI but not a priority). The Products scope suggests AI positioning IS the strategy, but buyer receptivity, competitive AI messaging, and readiness for Merlin deployment are completely unknown. Under-positioned risk: if Metso is AI-curious but Marc has not articulated the Merlin agentic negotiation wedge and I2O intelligence value, we may be losing to a competitor with simpler, safer messaging. Next move: discovery call to assess AI maturity ('What is your experience with AI in procurement? What outcomes do you expect from agentic automation?') and position Merlin's supplier-price-history negotiation agent as the Phase 1 proof point.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: No buyer expectations or decision criteria have been captured (Business_Requirements__c, Top_Challenges_Priorities__c, AI_Needs_in_RFP_Rating__c all null; zero Avoma calls). Products field (Merlin, Agentic AI, ANA) implies interest in procurement automation and AI, but we have no evidence of specific use cases, functional priorities, integration requirements, or AI maturity expectations. Fit is unvalidated. Next move: discovery to extract top 3-5 buyer requirements and map to Zycus strengths.

hard.ais_why:
* Metso is at an early, exploratory stage: the next step is a follow-up where the prospect "will get someone from his Procurement COE team" - the evaluation has not been scoped yet.
* The deal is an ANA/analytics-led opportunity with Merlin and Agentic AI in scope, so AI is within the intended footprint.
* AI/analytics modules are in scope (Merlin, Agentic AI, ANA) but no customer-side AI interest is evidenced for this opportunity; Qualified at 10%.

Ask Julio to introduce Marc to the Procurement COE lead and the budget owner in the first 14 days, and add those contacts to the opportunity contact roles once confirmed involved.

value.flag: Validate the AI/Merlin fit hypothesis and position the agentic negotiation story as the wedge if they prove AI-hungry. Products__c (Merlin;Agentic AI;ANA) reflects our AI-forward positioning, but we have not confirmed Metso values negotiation automation, has a negotiation pain Merlin solves, or sees AI as a differentiator vs a nice-to-have. Use the discovery call to probe: are you exploring AI for procurement, what negotiation challenges do you face (cycle time, win-rate, supplier push-back, cost leakage), and how do you currently handle complex multi-round negotiations. If they express AI appetite, lead with the Merlin agentic negotiation narrative (autonomous supplier price analysis, real-time counteroffer generation, learning from historical deals) and tie it to a quantified ROI (e.g. 15–20% faster negotiation cycles, 3–5% better outcomes). If AI is not a priority, reframe ANA as a best-practice sourcing analytics and category management platform and position Merlin as a future-proof add-on.
